scope.note: Single primitive — Create kind=contract. All files inside the WP Contract scope.

size.note: 133 lines are vendored schema (mechanical), 173 lines are JSON-LD data, 175 lines are test code + journal. No prose/code overlap.

safety.note: One JSON Schema vendored from upstream brain plugin (byte-identical to source); no new migrations, no infra, no secrets.

completeness.note: Data-only WP. The new JSON-LD instance has a dedicated test file with 6 tests covering parse/count/schema-validate/enum/lived-defects/naming.

checks_run: resilience scan (no new HTTP/RPC/DB calls; pure file reads)
observability scan (no new handlers/endpoints needing spans)
verification scan (data file backed by 6 unit tests including schema validation)

security.note: Static data + test diff with no untrusted input surfaces. No new dependencies (jsonschema + pytest already declared in pyproject).

findings.title: WP Contract table uses kind={operational,structural} + severity, but schema enum + schema-has-no-severity-field
findings.evidence: The WP Contract table lists kind=operational/structural and severity=low/medium/high/critical for each FailureMode. The foundation FailureMode schema has kind enum {timeout, dependency-unavailable, invalid-input, policy-violation, sanctions-hit, business-logic-error, infrastructure-failure, capacity-exceeded, authorisation-denied, other} and NO severity field at all (expected_frequency exists instead, enum {rare,occasional,frequent}).
findings.recommendation: Schema-canonical path taken: each FailureMode uses a schema-valid kind value chosen to best characterise the failure, and severity is dropped in favour of expected_frequency. The drift is documented in the journal's Step 1.5 approach summary and in the failuremodes.jsonld _about field. No fix needed in this WP. A follow-up housekeeping WP could update WP-004's Contract table to use schema-canonical vocabulary for posterity — but that's an editorial change to the WP file, not a code change.

quality.jsx_scan: N/A — no TSX/JSX files in diff
quality.test_coverage: 6 tests for 1 new data file + 1 vendored schema; all DoD checklist items covered
quality.perf_patterns_cr10: no anti-pattern matches — no loops with I/O, no N+1, no unbounded materialisation

verdict_reasoning: PASS: Build Verification empty, no critical/high in diff, all 3 lenses produced output, all files >50 lines read end-to-end (the executor authored them this session).
